rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical alkoxysulfonylation of α-substituted styrenes with sodium arenesulfinates and alcohols to give β-alkoxysulfones via radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sodium 4-methylbenzenesulfinate to a sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic C-radical adduct to a benzylic cation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct (C) from sulfonyl radical addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation (D) after anodic oxidation of C
protonated β-alkoxysulfone (E) after MeOH attack on D

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSO2Na to a sulfonyl radical (via O-radical tautomer), which adds to the styrene C=C to give a benzylic radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by methanol, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 3 equiv TEMPO completely inhibited the reaction (0% yield of 3a) with α-methylstyrene 1a largely recovered, indicating a radical pathway

notes: Competing hydroxysulfonylation (with water as nucleophile) is observed when isopropanol is used, lowering yield to 29%. Mechanism is proposed based on TEMPO inhibition plus literature precedent (refs 13b, 14, 15); no DFT, CV, or KIE reported.
